data.description:
This requires a body of water at least 25 feet wide and 50 feet deep. You cause a whirlpool in the area. The whirlpool forms a vortex that is 50 feet wide at the base, 25 feet tall at the top, and 5 feet deep.
Any object or creature within 50 feet of the center of the effect is pulled toward the vortex. The water in the area moves as directed, but it resumes its normal flow once it leaves the spell's area.

When a creature is pulled into the vortex, it can make a Strength (Athletics) check against your spell DC. On a failed save, the creature takes 2d8 bludgeoning damage and is caught in the vortex until the spell ends. If a creature or its pieces are within 30 feet of nonmagical ammunition when it enters the vortex on their first turn, they must make a Strength (Athletics) check to swim away from the vortex. On a failed save, the creature takes half damage and is caught in the vortex. A piece of ammunition that flies into the vortex deals piercing damage to any creature or object within 30 feet, taking 2d8 bludgeoning damage each round it remains in the vortex.

Casting Time: reaction, you when see a creature within 60 feet casting a weather spell within miles; take your action immediately to control the spell for duration. You must be outdoors to cast this spell. Range: 60 feet. Components: S. Duration: Instantaneous
